memes.label: Alan Breck Stewart
memes.description: Jacobite fugitive and co-protagonist of Stevenson's *Catriona*, friend and protector of David Balfour, hunted for his alleged role in the Appin Murder.

memes.label: Alison Hastie
memes.description: A young woman from Lime Kilns who helped Alan Breck and David Balfour cross the Firth of Forth in Kidnapped. Her quiet kindness leaves a lasting impression on David, and she reappears at the end of Catriona as a nursemaid to the Balfour children.

memes.label: Andie Dale
memes.description: A sailor and the keeper (Prefect) of the Bass Rock, where David Balfour is held prisoner. Known as "Black Andie," he serves as both jailer and companion to David during his captivity on the island.

memes.label: Appin
memes.description: A district in Argyllshire, in the West Highlands of Scotland — the territory of the Stewart clan and the site of the Appin Murder, the event that drives the plot of Catriona

memes.label: Appin Murder
memes.description: The assassination of Colin Campbell of Glenure in the wood of Lettermore, Appin, on 14 May 1752 — the event that drives the entire plot of Stevenson's *Catriona* and one of Scotland's most notorious unsolved murders.

memes.label: Balwhidder
memes.description: A Highland district in Perthshire, Scotland, referenced by David Balfour as the place from which he travelled to Edinburgh, marking his transition from country life to the city.

memes.label: Barbara Grant
memes.description: The sister of Lord Prestongrange and a friend to both David Balfour and Catriona Drummond — one of the few characters in Stevenson's *Catriona* who navigates the political divide with genuine kindness.

memes.label: Bass Rock
memes.description: A volcanic island in the Firth of Forth used as a prison, where David Balfour is "sequestered" by order of Prestongrange during the trial of James Stewart

memes.label: British Linen Company
memes.description: The Edinburgh commercial institution where David Balfour is employed before the events of *Catriona* — a symbol of the new, commercial Scotland emerging alongside the old clan order.

memes.label: Caddie
memes.description: Edinburgh guide/porter system in 18th century — essential to city navigation and central to several scenes in Catriona.

memes.label: Catriona Drummond
memes.description: The title character of Stevenson's *Catriona*, daughter of James More MacGregor, and the woman David Balfour loves — a Highland heroine of courage, honour, and fierce loyalty.

memes.label: Catriona
memes.description: Robert Louis Stevenson's 1893 novel, subtitled *A Sequel to "Kidnapped"*, continuing the story of David Balfour as he navigates the legal and political aftermath of the Appin Murder and falls in love with Catriona Drummond.

memes.label: "Chapter I: A BEGGAR ON HORSEBACK"
memes.description: "The 25th day of August, 1751, about two in the afternoon, I, David Balfour, came forth of the British Linen Company, a porter attending me with a bag of money, and some of the chief of these mercha..."

memes.label: "Chapter II: THE HIGHLAND WRITER"
memes.description: "Mr."

memes.label: "Chapter III: I GO TO PILRIG"
memes.description: "The next morning, I was no sooner awake in my new lodging than I was up and into my new clothes; and no sooner the breakfast swallowed, than I was forth on my adventurers."

memes.label: "Chapter IV: LORD ADVOCATE PRESTONGRANGE"
memes.description: "My kinsman kept me to a meal, “for the honour of the roof,” he said; and I believe I made the better speed on my return."

memes.label: "Chapter V: IN THE ADVOCATE’S HOUSE"
memes.description: "The next day, Sabbath, August 27th, I had the occasion I had long looked forward to, to hear some of the famous Edinburgh preachers, all well known to me already by the report of Mr Campbell."

memes.label: "Chapter VI: UMQUILE THE MASTER OF LOVAT"
memes.description: "There was a man waiting us in Prestongrange’s study, whom I distasted at the first look, as we distaste a ferret or an earwig."

memes.label: "Chapter VII: I MAKE A FAULT IN HONOUR"
memes.description: "I came forth, I vow I know not how, on the *Lang Dykes* [12]."

memes.label: "Chapter VIII: THE BRAVO"
memes.description: "The next day, August 29th, I kept my appointment at the Advocate’s in a coat that I had made to my own measure, and was but newly ready."

memes.label: "Chapter IX: THE HEATHER ON FIRE"
memes.description: "When I left Prestongrange that afternoon I was for the first time angry."

memes.label: "Chapter X: THE RED-HEADED MAN"
memes.description: "It was about half-past three when I came forth on the Lang Dykes."

memes.label: "Chapter XI: THE WOOD BY SILVERMILLS"
memes.description: "I lost no time, but down through the valley and by Stockbridge and Silvermills as hard as I could stave."

memes.label: "Chapter XII: ON THE MARCH AGAIN WITH ALAN"
memes.description: "It was likely between one and two; the moon (as I have said) was down; a strongish wind, carrying a heavy wrack of cloud, had set in suddenly from the west; and we began our movement in as black a ..."

memes.label: "Chapter XIII: GILLANE SANDS"
memes.description: "I did not profit by Alan’s pilotage as he had done by his marchings under General Cope; for I can scarce tell what way we went."

memes.label: "Chapter XIV: THE BASS"
memes.description: "I had no thought where they were taking me; only looked here and there for the appearance of a ship; and there ran the while in my head a word of Ransome’s—the*twenty-pounders*."

memes.label: "Chapter XV: BLACK ANDIE’S TALE OF TOD LAPRAIK"
memes.description: "I have yet said little of the Highlanders."

memes.label: "Chapter XVI: THE MISSING WITNESS"
memes.description: "On the seventeenth, the day I was trysted with the Writer, I had much rebellion against fate."

memes.label: "Chapter XVII: THE MEMORIAL"
memes.description: "The last word of the blessing was scarce out of the minister’s mouth before Stewart had me by the arm."

memes.label: "Chapter XVIII: THE TEE’D BALL"
memes.description: "On the morrow, from the justices’ private room, where none could see me, I heard the verdict given in and judgment rendered upon James."

memes.label: "Chapter XIX: I AM MUCH IN THE HANDS OF THE LADIES"
memes.description: "The copying was a weary business, the more so as I perceived very early there was no sort of urgency in the matters treated, and began very early to consider my employment a pretext."

memes.label: "Chapter XX: I CONTINUE TO MOVE IN GOOD SOCIETY"
memes.description: "For about exactly two months I remained a guest in Prestongrange’s family, where I bettered my acquaintance with the bench, the bar, and the flower of Edinburgh company."

memes.label: "Chapter XXI: THE VOYAGE INTO HOLLAND"
memes.description: "The ship lay at a single anchor, well outside the pier of Leith, so that all we passengers must come to it by the means of skiffs."

memes.label: "Chapter XXII: HELVOETSLUYS"
memes.description: "The weather in the end considerably worsened; the wind sang in the shrouds, the sea swelled higher, and the ship began to labour and cry out among the billows."

memes.label: "Chapter XXIII: TRAVELS IN HOLLAND"
memes.description: "The rattel-waggon, which is a kind of a long waggon set with benches, carried us in four hours of travel to the great city of Rotterdam."

memes.label: "Chapter XXIV: FULL STORY OF A COPY OF HEINECCIUS"
memes.description: "The place found was in the upper part of a house backed on a canal."

memes.label: "Chapter XXV: THE RETURN OF JAMES MORE"
memes.description: "I was called on the morrow out of a late and troubled slumber by a knocking on my door, ran to open it, and had almost swooned with the contrariety of my feelings, mostly painful; for on the thresh..."

memes.label: "Chapter XXVI: THE THREESOME"
memes.description: "Whether or not I was to be so much blamed, or rather perhaps pitied, I must leave others to judge."

memes.label: "Chapter XXVII: A TWOSOME"
memes.description: "I believe it was about the fifth day, and I know at least that James was in one of his fits of gloom, when I received three letters."

memes.label: "Chapter XXVIII: IN WHICH I AM LEFT ALONE"
memes.description: "I opened the door to Catriona and stopped her on the threshold."

memes.label: "Chapter XXIX: WE MEET IN DUNKIRK"
memes.description: "Altogether, then, I was scare so miserable the next days but what I had many hopeful and happy snatches; threw myself with a good deal of constancy upon my studies; and made out to endure the time ..."

memes.label: "Chapter XXX: THE LETTER FROM THE SHIP"
memes.description: "Daylight showed us how solitary the inn stood."

memes.label: Charles Edward Stuart
memes.description: Bonnie Prince Charlie — Jacobite claimant to the British throne, leader of the 1745 Rising, referenced throughout Catriona.
aliases: 查尔斯·爱德华·斯图亚特

memes.label: Charles Stewart the Writer
memes.description: Edinburgh lawyer and Stewart clan agent, the first person David Balfour visits in Edinburgh — a key intermediary between the Highland fugitives and the legal establishment in Stevenson's *Catriona*.

memes.label: Clan Campbell
memes.description: The dominant Whig clan of 18th-century Scotland — led by the Duke of Argyll, the Campbells were the government's Highland enforcers and the Stewarts' bitter rivals

memes.label: Clan Macgregor
memes.description: The most notorious of the proscribed Highland clans — the Macgregors of the Gregara — whose name was outlawed after 1603 and whose members include James More and Catriona

memes.label: Clan System
memes.description: The Highland clan system in 18th-century Scotland — kinship-based social and political organization central to the events of Catriona

memes.label: Cluny Macpherson
memes.description: The chief of Clan Macpherson and a prominent Jacobite leader who sheltered Alan Breck Stewart and David Balfour in his famous hiding-place, Cluny's Cage, after the Battle of Culloden.

memes.label: Colin Campbell of Glenure
memes.description: The government factor on the forfeited Stewart estates, whose assassination in the wood of Lettermore on 14 May 1752 — the Appin Murder — sets the entire plot of Stevenson's *Catriona* in motion.

memes.label: 概念索引
memes.description: 《Catriona》小说及相关历史语境中的全部概念词条索引。

memes.label: "Conclusion"
memes.description: "Conclusion of Catriona"

memes.label: David Balfour
memes.description: The narrator and protagonist of Catriona — a young Lowland Whig, newly inherited laird of Shaws, and law student caught between Hanoverian justice and Highland loyalty

memes.label: "Dedication"
memes.description: "Dedication to Charles Baxter, Writer to the Signet"

memes.label: Disarming Act
memes.description: Post-1745 Highland disarmament legislation, key to clan political context in Catriona.

memes.label: Duncan Dhu Maclaren
memes.description: A Highland friend mentioned early in Stevenson's *Catriona* — a link to David Balfour's adventures in *Kidnapped* and a representative of the network of Highland allies who sustain the novel's fugitive characters.

memes.label: Dunkirk
memes.description: French port town where David Balfour reunites with Catriona and Alan Breck Stewart in January 1752, serving as the setting for the novel's climax.

memes.label: Edinburgh
memes.description: The capital of Scotland and the primary setting of Catriona — the city of the law courts, the Advocate's chambers, and the streets where David Balfour meets Catriona Drummond

memes.label: "Editions Note"
memes.description: "Note about available editions of this ebook"

memes.label: 事件索引
memes.description: 《Catriona》小说中涉及的全部事件索引，包括政治事件、法律事件与私人事件。

memes.label: "Footnotes"
memes.description: "Footnotes for Catriona"

memes.label: "Front Matter"
memes.description: "Project Gutenberg front matter and metadata for Catriona"

memes.label: Gillane Sands
memes.description: Remote beach on the East Lothian coast where Alan Breck Stewart was to be picked up by Andie Scougal's ship — a rendezvous that ended in betrayal and armed confrontation.

memes.label: Glengyle Macgregor
memes.description: Macgregor chieftain of the Glengyle branch, Catriona rode beside him in the 1745 Rising (Catriona ch-10, ch-21).

memes.label: "The Full Project Gutenberg License"
memes.description: "Full text of the Project Gutenberg license"

memes.label: Hector Duncansby
memes.description: Highland officer sent to duel David Balfour at Hope Park in Catriona ch-08 — a comic, honor-bound figure.

memes.label: Heineccius
memes.description: A substantial law book by the German jurist Dr. Heineccius, which David Balfour studies while at Leyden University and uses as a pretext to avoid intimacy with Catriona.

memes.label: Helvoetsluys
memes.description: Dutch port town where David Balfour and Catriona Drummond land after crossing from Scotland, and where Catriona is to meet her father James More before departing for exile in France.

memes.label: Hereditable Jurisdiction
memes.description: The system of private courts and legal powers held by Scottish chiefs and lairds, abolished after the 1745 Rising — a key legal concept underlying the Appin Murder trial in Catriona

memes.label: Highland Line
memes.description: The cultural and linguistic boundary separating the Scottish Highlands from the Lowlands, a divide that shapes identity, law, and politics throughout Catriona

memes.label: Inverary
memes.description: The head burgh of the Campbells in Argyll, Scotland, where James Stewart of the Glens was tried for the Appin Murder in September 1752.

memes.label: James More Macgregor
memes.description: Catriona's father, a Macgregor chieftain, Jacobite soldier, and political prisoner whose shifting loyalties drive much of the novel's plot

memes.label: James Stewart of the Glens
memes.description: Stewart clansman and half-brother to Ardshiel, wrongfully tried and hanged for the Appin Murder — the case David Balfour fights to overturn in Stevenson's *Catriona*.

memes.label: Kidnapped
memes.description: Robert Louis Stevenson's 1886 adventure novel chronicling David Balfour's kidnapping, shipwreck, and flight through the Highlands with Alan Breck Stewart — the predecessor to *Catriona*.

memes.label: King's Park
memes.description: Rough rocky ground near Holyrood in Edinburgh containing Hope Park (a formal pleasure garden) and the Hunter's Bog — site of David Balfour's farcical duel with Lieutenant Hector Duncansby.

memes.label: Laird
memes.description: The Scottish landed gentry — a laird was a landowner of middling rank below a lord but above a tenant farmer, a social position central to David Balfour's identity in Catriona

memes.label: Lettermore
memes.description: Location in Appin, Argyll — the wood where Colin Campbell of Glenure was shot (the Appin Murder) and where James of the Glens was later hanged.

memes.label: Leyden
memes.description: The Dutch university city where Alan Breck Stewart plans his escape and where David Balfour studies law during his exile — the European counterpoint to Edinburgh in Stevenson's *Catriona*.

memes.label: Lime Kilns
memes.description: Village on the Firth of Forth where Alison Hastie lived — the girl who helped Alan Breck and David Balfour cross the Forth during their flight after the Appin murder.

memes.label: Lord Advocate Office
memes.description: The office of Lord Advocate — Scotland's chief legal officer, central to the political intrigue in Catriona.
aliases: 苏格兰总检察长办公室

memes.label: Lord Prestongrange
memes.description: The Lord Advocate of Scotland — William Grant of Prestongrange — the powerful legal officer who prosecutes James Stewart while maneuvering to protect David Balfour

memes.label: Luckenbooths
memes.description: Historic merchant quarter on Edinburgh's Royal Mile where David Balfour purchased his city clothes and sword upon arriving from the country.

memes.label: Mr. Balfour of Pilrig
memes.description: David Balfour's Whig kinsman who wrote a crucial letter of introduction to Prestongrange (Catriona ch-03).
aliases: 皮尔里格的巴尔弗先生

memes.label: Mr. Campbell (Preacher)
memes.description: Minister from Essendean, David Balfour's childhood preceptor, mentioned in Catriona ch-01 and ch-05.

memes.label: Mrs. Drummond-Ogilvy of Allardyce
memes.description: Catriona's aristocratic host in Dean Village, who provided her shelter and social standing in Edinburgh society.
aliases: 德拉蒙德-奥格尔维夫人

memes.label: Musselburgh
memes.description: Town east of Edinburgh where David Balfour and Alan Breck Stewart breakfasted on haddock during their march eastward away from the city.

memes.label: Parliament House
memes.description: The seat of the Court of Session and the centre of legal Edinburgh in the 18th century — the institutional stage on which the legal drama of Stevenson's *Catriona* is performed.

memes.label: Peden the Prophet
memes.description: Covenanter preacher imprisoned on Bass Rock, subject of Black Andie's supernatural tale in Catriona ch-15.

memes.label: 人物索引
memes.description: 《Catriona》小说及相关历史背景中的全部人物索引，按姓名字母排序。

memes.label: 地点索引
memes.description: 《Catriona》小说及相关历史背景中的全部地点词条索引，按名称排序。

memes.label: Mr. Rankeillor
memes.description: A lawyer (Writer) in Queensferry who serves as the legal agent for the Shaws estate. He assisted David Balfour in recovering his inheritance in Kidnapped and continues to advise him through the events of Catriona.

memes.label: Rob Roy Macgregor
memes.description: Legendary Macgregor clan leader and outlaw, father of James More Drummond, referenced throughout Catriona.

memes.label: Robin Oig Macgregor
memes.description: Catriona's uncle, James More's brother — a Macgregor clansman referenced in Catriona ch-01 and ch-21.

memes.label: Royal Pardon
memes.description: The sovereign power to remit punishment for crimes — the instrument around which James More MacGregor's treachery, Lord Prestongrange's authority, and the fate of Jacobite fugitives revolve in Stevenson's *Catriona*.

memes.label: Scots Law
memes.description: The distinct legal system of Scotland, the institutional framework within which the Appin Murder trial, David Balfour's legal education, and the political machinations of *Catriona* unfold.

memes.label: Scottish Highlands
memes.description: The mountainous northern region of Scotland, homeland of the Highland clans and the primary setting of Stevenson's Highland novels — a landscape of heather, exile, and clan loyalty.

memes.label: Sequestration on the Bass Rock
memes.description: The government's confinement of David Balfour on the Bass Rock to prevent his testimony from aiding James Stewart of the Glens — a pivotal political event in Stevenson's *Catriona*.

memes.label: Sheriff Erskine
memes.description: Sheriff of Perth, a conspirator in the Appin murder case present at Prestongrange's house in Catriona ch-08.

memes.label: Silvermills
memes.description: Village near Edinburgh on the Water of Leith where Alan Breck Stewart hid while watching over David Balfour's affairs in the city.

memes.label: The 1745 Rising
memes.description: The Jacobite rebellion of 1745–1746 led by Charles Edward Stuart — the defining political-military event of mid-18th-century Scotland, whose aftermath shapes every character's world in Catriona

memes.label: The Grant Daughters
memes.description: Prestongrange's three daughters — social-intrigue figures in Catriona ch-05 and ch-19–20, representing Edinburgh high society.

memes.label: The Rose (ship)
memes.description: A merchant vessel captained by Captain Sang, which carried David Balfour and Catriona Drummond from Leith to Holland. The ship's voyage across the North Sea forms the setting for the development of David and Catriona's relationship.

memes.label: The Seahorse (warship)
memes.description: A Royal Navy warship under Captain Palliser, stationed in the Firth of Forth. The ship appears at key moments in Catriona — first firing on the Bass Rock, and later off the coast of France in a significant plot development.

memes.label: The Thristle
memes.description: A merchant ship captained by Andie Scougal, engaged to carry Alan Breck Stewart to France in the novel Catriona. Referenced in early chapters as part of the escape planning.

memes.label: Tod Lapraik
memes.description: Subject of Black Andie's supernatural tale aboard the Bass Rock voyage — a folklore figure appearing in Catriona ch-15.

memes.label: 核心词条（被引排行）
memes.description: 按被引用数排序的核心词条 Top 50，反映全书叙事中最重要的实体。

memes.label: Trial of James Stewart
memes.description: The 1752 trial of James Stewart of the Glens for the Appin Murder — a political prosecution before a Campbell-packed jury that David Balfour risks everything to testify against
aliases: trial of James of the Glens

memes.label: Whig and Jacobite
memes.description: The political and religious divide between Whigs (Hanoverian, Presbyterian, pro-Union) and Jacobites (Stuart loyalist, Episcopalian/Catholic, pro-independence) in 18th-century Scotland, the central political tension of Catriona
